search_summary.key_focus: KenPom data access for Python sports betting model (2020-2025 historical data)

restrictions: Limited to fixed number of physical locations/machines

official_api.data_usage_terms: Not publicly documented - must review after API key purchase
official_api.documentation: Provided via link after API key purchase

csv_export.note: No direct CSV export feature documented. Must use API or web scraping.

point_in_time_ratings.note: Critical for betting models - ratings BEFORE games, not after

core_metrics.name: AdjO
core_metrics.definition: Points scored per 100 possessions, adjusted for opponent defense quality
core_metrics.predictive_value: HIGH - core predictor of offensive performance

core_metrics.name: AdjD
core_metrics.definition: Points allowed per 100 possessions, adjusted for opponent offense quality
core_metrics.predictive_value: HIGH - core predictor of defensive performance

core_metrics.name: AdjEM
core_metrics.definition: Difference between AdjO and AdjD (AdjO - AdjD)
core_metrics.interpretation: Higher is better - represents expected margin vs average D-I team
core_metrics.predictive_value: VERY HIGH - single best team strength metric
core_metrics.advantage: Linear measure - easy to compare teams

core_metrics.name: AdjT
core_metrics.definition: Possessions per 40 minutes, adjusted for opponent

four_factors.description: Dean Oliver's Four Factors - fundamental efficiency components

factors.name: eFG%
factors.definition: Field goal percentage adjusted for 3-pointers being worth more
factors.why_predictive: Shooting efficiency is the most important offensive skill

factors.name: TOV%
factors.definition: Percentage of possessions ending in turnover
factors.why_predictive: Turnovers end possessions without shot attempt

factors.name: ORB%
factors.definition: Percentage of available offensive rebounds grabbed

factors.name: FTR
factors.definition: Free throw attempts per field goal attempt
factors.why_predictive: Free throws are high-efficiency scoring opportunities

four_factors.application: Available for both offense and defense - creates 8 total factors

calculation_methodology.recency_weighting: More weight on recent games (exact formula proprietary)
calculation_methodology.opponent_adjustment: All games factored to determine relative team strength

kenpompy.citation: J. Andrews. 'kenpompy: A simple yet comprehensive web scraper for kenpom.com.' GitHub, v0.3.5. https://github.com/j-andrews7/kenpompy
kenpompy.last_update: No major release in last 12 months

cloudflare_blocking.description: Cloudflare protection blocks requests from AWS/Azure/VPNs
cloudflare_blocking.workaround: Use residential IP or browser automation libraries

pandas_deprecation.description: Passing literal HTML to read_html deprecated in pandas 2.1.0+

current_status_2026.reliability: LOW - frequent failures due to Cloudflare
current_status_2026.recommendation: NOT RECOMMENDED for production use in 2026
current_status_2026.alternatives: Use hoopR (R), cbbdata (R), or official API

kenpompy.usage_example: import kenpompy.utils as utils\nbrowser = utils.login(email, password)\nratings = kenpompy.summary.get_pomeroy_ratings(browser, season='2025')

hoopR_py.citation: S. Gilani. 'hoopR-py: Access men's basketball play by play data.' GitHub. https://github.com/saiemgilani/hoopR-py
hoopR_py.note: Does NOT provide KenPom ratings, but excellent for raw game data

sportsdataverse_py.citation: SportsDataverse. 'sportsdataverse-py (sdv-py): Python sports data library.' GitHub. https://sportsdataverse-py.sportsdataverse.org/
sportsdataverse_py.note: Broader sports coverage than hoopR, but no KenPom data

cbbpy.citation: CBBpy contributors. 'CBBpy: Python-based web scraper for NCAA basketball.' PyPI. https://pypi.org/project/CBBpy/
cbbpy.note: Scrapes NCAA.org, not KenPom - useful for raw stats

data_access_packages.name: toRvik
data_available: Player game-by-game stats (70,000+ games back to 2008)

data_access_packages.name: cbbdata
data_available: Game-by-game player box scores and advanced metrics (back to 2008)
data_access_packages.note: Faster and more efficient than toRvik

similarities: Adjusted efficiency metrics (AdjO, AdjD, AdjEM, AdjT)
differences: Recency bias: Games lose 1% emphasis after 40 days, fully aged out at 80 days
Women's basketball coverage (KenPom is men's only)
comparison_to_kenpom.correlation: VERY HIGH - ratings typically within 1-2 points for most teams
comparison_to_kenpom.betting_value: Equivalent to KenPom for most purposes

workarounds: Use rpy2 to call R packages from Python
Request cbbdata API directly via HTTP (Flask backend)
Scrape barttorvik.com (not recommended - ToS violation likely)
python_access.recommendation: Use rpy2 + toRvik or cbbdata for best results

methodology.description: Best predictor of team performance going forward

advantages: Includes game predictions and win probabilities
disadvantages: Less granular than KenPom (no Four Factors)
No historical archives beyond current season
API is undocumented and may change
comparison_to_kenpom.correlation: HIGH - BPI correlates strongly with KenPom AdjEM
comparison_to_kenpom.betting_value: Good for quick assessments, less detail than KenPom

python_access.method: ESPN Core API via hoopR-py or direct HTTP requests
python_access.example: See espn_ncaab_fetcher.py in project for implementation

ncaa_stats_api.name: NCAA Stats API (Community)

packages.name: NCAA-API (dwillis)
packages.method: Python application to turn NCAA web stats into API

ncaa_stats_api.use_case: Raw box score data for computing your own efficiency metrics

computing_custom_metrics.feasibility: YES - Possible to calculate Pomeroy-style metrics from raw data

possessions.note: 0.475 is consensus for college basketball, 0.44 is Dean Oliver's original NBA formula

steps: 1. Calculate raw efficiency for every game
2. Set up system of equations: each team's efficiency = rating + opponent rating + error
3. Solve using least squares to minimize total error
4. Apply recency weights (more recent games weighted higher)
adjusted_efficiency.complexity: MEDIUM - requires linear algebra and optimization
adjusted_efficiency.implementation: Similar to Elo rating system but uses regression instead of updating

tempo.adjustment: Calculate average tempo vs each opponent, then regress to find team tempo independent of opponent

simple_version.use_case: Quick baseline, not suitable for betting
simple_version.example: Calculate points per 100 possessions for each team across all games

intermediate_version.method: Start with simple average, then iteratively adjust for opponent strength
intermediate_version.accuracy: Will correlate 0.85-0.90 with KenPom if done correctly

advanced_version.what: Full least squares with recency weights and home court adjustment

recommendation.for_this_project: START with existing Elo model, ADD Four Factors as features
rationale: Four Factors can be computed from ESPN box scores (already fetching)
Adding features is lower effort than rebuilding rating system
Can compare Elo vs Efficiency-based models later
recommendation.future_enhancement: If Elo+Four Factors plateaus, implement full adjusted efficiency

common_patterns: R packages dominate college basketball analytics (toRvik, cbbdata, hoopR)
Python ecosystem lags behind R for CBB data access
Web scraping is fragile due to Cloudflare and site changes
ESPN hidden APIs are most reliable free source
KenPom subscription is worth it if serious about betting
best_practices: Use official APIs (ESPN Core API) over web scraping when possible
Implement rate limiting (2 req/sec) for undocumented APIs
Cache data locally to minimize API calls
Validate data quality - check for missing games or anomalous values
Use point-in-time ratings (before game) not end-of-season ratings
pitfalls: Look-ahead bias: Using post-game ratings for prediction
Treating all possessions equally (top teams are more consistent)
Ignoring tempo when predicting point spreads
Not accounting for home court advantage (~3-4 points)
Missing garbage time adjustments (blowouts skew efficiency)
emerging_trends: Shift from web scraping to API-first approaches
Women's basketball analytics catching up to men's

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Need KenPom data with minimal development effort
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Official API is most reliable, cost is negligible for betting project, saves 20+ hours development
implementation_steps: 3. Build simple Python wrapper for API endpoints

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Want free alternative with similar predictive power
implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Use Barttorvik via toRvik (R) + rpy2 (Python bridge)
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Free, actively maintained, correlates highly with KenPom, extensive historical data
implementation_steps: 3. Write Python wrapper to call R functions

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Want to compute custom efficiency metrics
implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Use ESPN box scores + implement simplified adjusted efficiency
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Already fetching ESPN data, full control over methodology, educational value
implementation_steps: 1. Extend espn_ncaab_fetcher.py to pull box scores

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Want best of both worlds (free + reliable)
implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Use ESPN BPI + Barttorvik + Four Factors as ensemble
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Diversifies data sources, combines free ratings with custom features, reduces single-point-of-failure risk
implementation_steps: 1. Fetch ESPN BPI ratings via Core API
3. Compute Four Factors from ESPN box scores
4. Create ensemble features: BPI_rating, Barttorvik_rating, Elo_rating, Four_Factors

popular_solutions: KenPom subscription for serious bettors ($24.95/year is standard)
Barttorvik via toRvik for R users and academic research
ESPN data for casual analysis and free projects
Hybrid approaches combining multiple data sources
controversial_topics: KenPom vs Barttorvik: Which is 'better'? (Consensus: negligible difference)
Web scraping ethics: Legal but against ToS for most sites
Recency weighting: How much to emphasize recent games?
Garbage time adjustments: Should blowout possessions be downweighted?
expert_opinions: Ken Pomeroy: 'Efficiency margin is the single best predictor of team strength'
Dean Oliver: 'Shooting (eFG%) is 40% of winning, twice as important as free throws'
Bart Torvik: 'Recency bias (decay after 40 days) improves prediction accuracy'
Betting community: 'CLV is more important than win rate - get better odds than closing lines'

phase_1_immediate.rationale: Fastest path to high-quality data, cost is minimal, saves significant development time

phase_2_short_term.action: Add Four Factors as features to existing Elo model
phase_2_short_term.rationale: Already have ESPN box score capability, Four Factors are proven predictors, low effort high reward

phase_3_medium_term.action: Implement Barttorvik data fetcher via toRvik as free backup
phase_3_medium_term.rationale: Reduces dependence on single data source, provides validation dataset

phase_4_long_term.action: Build custom adjusted efficiency calculator for full control
phase_4_long_term.rationale: Educational value, complete independence from external services, can experiment with modifications
